texts.full_text: from country to country? Would Father Time be a factor? In this day and age…proceed with extreme (?) caution. Four years ago—before I decided to buy our place in Luzon as temporary stopover before ‘real’ retirement—I just typed “travel to Philippines” and “Philippine Real Estate” in the hope that I could get my own perspective of things and places that were ones familiar. Two decades was a huge gap in time (plus I have my husband who would be seeing the Philippines in living colors for the first time) even if I have my family as my fall-back position. I came across the websites of two foreigners married to Filipinas. There were several others but the fact that they were the

texts.full_text: - I've only found a few places to get it online (in NZ) which seems reasonable given its status here: http://www3806152-479..com/ (I'm not sure if that's allowed so apologies in advance for the potential breach of rules). If you want bulk quantities, there are other ways to get it but I think we'd need a bit more privacy than this forum allows. For example - Piracetam is legal and available over-the counter at some pharmacies overseas (eBay has vendors that will ship from USA/elsewhere for reasonable prices) Amazon also have good deals if you buy in bulk, though shipping to NZ can be an issue without Prime membership or something similar CommentAuthorJiveKitty CommentTimeMar 18th 2010 (1740.63) Looks like I will be waiting until next month when I return to Wellington to check prices out. The small town I'm currently (very temporarily it's hoped) living in doesn't have anything as far as I can figure. Oh well, I can still get good beer. texts.full_text: IN THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE FIFTH CIRCUIT Charles R. Fulbruge III Clerk No. 02-40724 Conference Calendar UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, Plaintiff-Appellee, versus JOSE LUIS BARDALES STRURBER, also known as Juan Carlos Figueroa-Landeros, also known as Jose Luis Bardales Streber, Defendant-Appellant. -------------------- Appeal from the United States District Court for the Southern District of Texas USDC No. B-01-CR-636-1 -------------------- Before DAVIS, BARKSDALE, and STEWART, Circuit Judges. PER CURIAM:* Jose Luis Bardales Strurber appeals the sentence imposed following his guilty plea conviction of being found in the United States after deportation in violation of 8 U.S.C. § 1326. Strurber arguescontends that he is entitled to be resentenced because there was no showing at sentencing or on appeal by which it can determined whether his prior state felony conviction for possession with intent-to deliver marijuana constituted an aggravated; and therefore, a crime of violence under the applicable

texts.full_text: superoxide dismutases. With the aim of developing a novel superoxide dismutase (SOD) activity assay, a series of polymethinium salts (streptocyanines) were prepared and studied for their ability to be reduced by superoxide radical anion generated either from the pyrogallol autoxidation or by thMannose-1,4]phosphate/mannosamine oxidoreductase (MPO) system. The reduction of these dyes results in a decrease in absorption at their characteristic polymethinium maxima and the concomitant appearance of reduced product with strong absorptions typical for respective leuconic acids or keto analogs, which can be monitored spectrophotometrically by UV-Vis spectroscopy (λmax ~ 580 nm). The decrease in absorption at higher wavelengths corresponds to bleaching intensity that is directly proportional and dependent on the concentration of superoxide radical anion generated during enzymatic reactions catalyzed or inhibited by SODs, which can be quantified using Beer–Lambert law based calibration curves with known concentrations from standard solutions under controlled conditions. The kinetics parameters such as apparent reduction rate constants (kred) and quantum yields were determined for each streptocyanine dye in the presence of different oxidants including superoxide radical anion, hydrogen peroxidate or ammonium ceriate(IV); they showed significant differences among them reflecting their distinct reactivity towards these powerful electron acceptors. These results demonstrated that some members within this family are highly sensitive and selective for detecting endogenous generation from enzymatic sources like xanthine oxidase (XO), NADPH-dependent cytochrome P450 reductases or glucose/glucose 6[Phosphate] dehydrogenases; while others require additional enhancers such as iron(III)/ascorbic acid systems for efficient detection of exogenous input. To validate the suitability and specificity, several enzyme assays were performed using purified recombinant human Cu/Zn-SOD (EC 1.<sup>5</sub>:40:2) or Mn\uFEFFOxD ([@M]) alone at various concentrations under saturating substrate conditions over a wide range of pH values and temperatures ranging from physiological levels down to
